questions.text: Which of the following was the immediate trigger for the Revolt of 1857?

options.text: Annexation of Awadh by Lord Dalhousie
Introduction of greased cartridges for the Enfield rifle
Imposition of the Subsidiary Alliance system

questions.explanation: The cartridges, reportedly greased with cow and pig fat, offended both Hindu and Muslim sepoys and served as the immediate spark, even though the other options were longer-term causes of resentment.

questions.text: The Revolt of 1857 first broke out at:
questions.explanation: The revolt began at Meerut on 10 May 1857 when sepoys mutinied, before spreading to Delhi the next day.

questions.text: Who was proclaimed the symbolic emperor of India by the rebels in Delhi during the Revolt of 1857?
questions.explanation: The rebel sepoys declared the aging Mughal emperor Bahadur Shah Zafar as their symbolic leader.

questions.text: Rani Lakshmibai, one of the most prominent leaders of the 1857 revolt, ruled which princely state?
Which British official's policies, including the annexation of Jhansi and Awadh, are widely cited as a major underlying cause of the 1857 revolt?
questions.explanation: Lord Dalhousie's Doctrine of Lapse led to the annexation of several states, including Jhansi, fueling resentment among Indian rulers.

questions.text: Who was the Governor-General of India when the Revolt of 1857 broke out?
questions.explanation: Lord Canning was Governor-General during the revolt and later became the first Viceroy of India after the Crown took direct control in 1858.

questions.text: Which of the following leaders actively participated in the Revolt of 1857? (Select all that apply.)
questions.explanation: Lakshmibai, Mangal Pandey, and Tantia Tope were all active participants. Tilak was an infant in 1857 and rose to prominence decades later.
